test_text.title: Man Inquires about Kingswell Sports Club Membership and Facilities

audioscript.text: Section 1, you will hear a man telephoning a sports club to ask about membership and facilities.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will see that there is an example that has been done for you.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: On this occasion only, the conversation relating to this will be played first.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good morning, oh, sorry, it's gone 12.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I'll start again.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Uh. Good afternoon, Kingswell Sports Club.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: How can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Oh, good afternoon.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I was wondering if you could give me some information about membership and facilities.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: The name of the sports club is Kingswell.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: So, Kingswell has been written in the space.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now we shall begin.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You should answer the questions as you listen, because you will not hear the recording a second time.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Listen carefully, and answer questions 1 to 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good morning, oh, sorry, it's gone 12.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I'll start again.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Uh. Good afternoon, Kingswell Sports Club.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: How can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Oh, good afternoon.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I was wondering if you could give me some information about membership and facilities.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Of course.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: What would you like to know?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Do you have tennis courts for example?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: No, I'm afraid we don't.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: We're primarily a golf club.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: What about football?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I heard you had a team.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: No, I'm sorry.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Perhaps you're thinking about Fresham Sports Center.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Oh, right.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I know it.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I've played badminton there.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Have you?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: They've got a lot of facilities we don't have, and vice versa.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: We do have a keep fit studio, which is very popular with members, and then as well as that, there's swimming of course.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: That's good, I like to swim every day.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: We have a range of classes too.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Do you have judo classes?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I'm keen to learn.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Well, at the moment, we offer kickboxing.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: We're planning to add judo and stretch classes soon.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: We're currently running a range of yoga classes too.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: What about relaxing after exercise?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I assume you have a restaurant or something.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: At the moment, we've got a salad bar, which is very popular.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: We'll also have a fully licensed restaurant by the end of the year.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Sounds good.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 5 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 5 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: What kind of membership are you interested in?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Um. Not really sure.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Uh. What are the options?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Well, there are 3 different membership schemes.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I see.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: What's the difference?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Well, the first one's called Gold, and you can use all the facilities at any time of the day or week.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: You can also join in as many classes as you like for free.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: That sounds good.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Is it very expensive?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Well, you pay a £250 joining fee.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: And then it's £450 - oh, no, I'm sorry, it's just gone up by £50.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Uh. Sorry about that.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: It's now £500 for the annual subscription fee.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Right, got that.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: And what's the next type?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Well, that's Silver, it's the same as Gold, except you have to pay a small fee of £1 per lesson for any you do.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: And you can only use the center at certain times.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I see.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: So when exactly?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: You can only use the facilities between 10 am and 4:30 pm.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: So I couldn't use the pool at 8 in the morning or evening then.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: That's right.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: OK, and the price for that is the joining fee the same as for gold?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Actually it's slightly less than the £250.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: It's £225, but the annual fee is only £300. Does that sound more like what you want?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Well, it's still rather more expensive than I thought.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I'm a student here in England, and I'm only here for 6 months.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Ah. Then the Bronze scheme would probably suit you best.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Uh. How is that different?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Well, some of the facilities have restricted use.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: And do I have to pay for classes?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Yes, it's £3 for each class you join.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I see.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: And what are the hours then?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Between 10:30 and 3:30 weekdays only, and you pay a £50 joining fee.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: The annual fee is £180 it works out at £15 a month, so that would be quite a lot cheaper.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Oh, that should be all right.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I could come in my free periods.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: What do I have to do if I want to join?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Well, we book you in for an assessment with an instructor, who will show you how to use all the equipment.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: If you want to organize a trial session and look around the center, you'll need to speak to David Kynchley.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Hmm. Could you spell that please?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Yes, David, KYNCHLEY.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I'll give you his direct line number.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Thanks.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Thank you for calling Kingswell Sports Club.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

test_text.title: Rivenden City Theatre's Reopening and Updates

audioscript.text: Section 2, you will hear a local radio broadcast about the Rivenden City Theater.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 11 to 16.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully, and answer questions 11 to 16.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: And here on Radio Rivenden, we have Lynne Rawley, the public relations officer of our own Rivenden City Theater.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Hello, Lynne.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Hello.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: Now the theater is reopening soon after its three-year redevelopment program, isn't it?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: That's right, and there are a lot of improvements.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: The first thing people will see when they go in is that the foyer has been repainted in the original green and gold, then the box office has been reoriented with its own access from the side of the building, instead of through the foyer.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: Which means it can be open longer hours, and has more space too.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: The shop that used to be in the foyer, which sold books and CDs, is the one part of the redevelopment which isn't yet complete.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: The plan is to find new premises for it near the theater, and we've had difficulty finding somewhere suitable.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: We hope to reopen the shop in the next few months.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: Will audiences find any difference in the auditorium?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Yes, we've increased the legroom between the rows.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: Hmm.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: This means that there are now fewer seats, but we're sure audiences will be much happier.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: And we've installed air conditioning, so it won't get so hot and stuffy.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: We already had a few seats which were suitable for wheelchair users.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: And now there are twice as many which we hope will meet demand.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: Something else that will benefit audiences is the new lifts.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: The two we used to have were very small and slow.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: They've now gone, and we've got much more efficient ones.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: Anything for the performers?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Yes, we've made a number of improvements backstage.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: The small dark dressing rooms we used to have have been converted into two large airy rooms, so they're much more comfortable now.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: And the state-of-the-art electronic sound and lighting systems have been installed.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the talk, you have some time to look at questions 17 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 17 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: OK, so what's the first play that audiences can see when the theater reopens?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: We've got a very exciting production of Peter Shaffer's Royal Hunt of the Sun, which is currently touring the country.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: That starts on October the 13th and runs till the 19th.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: We're experimenting a bit with the time the curtain goes up.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: We used to start all our performances at 7:30, but that made it difficult for people to go home by public transport.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: So instead, we're beginning at 7.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: Because at 9:45 when it finishes, there are still buses running.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: Uh huh.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: Tickets are already selling fast, the Friday and Saturday performances sold out almost immediately.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: And in fact now there are only tickets for Monday and Thursday.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: Uh. How much are they?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: We've introduced a simpler price structure.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: Ticket prices used to range from £6 to £30, but now they're all £18.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: They're available from the box office, in person, by phone, fax or post, or online.
audioscript.speaker: LYNNE

audioscript.text: OK, Lynne.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Now, if you'd like to give the contact details for the theater, so that our listeners...
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: Something else that will benefit audiences is the new lifts.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: The two we used to have were very small and slow.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: They've now gone, and we've got much more efficient ones.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: Anything for the performers?
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Yes, we've made a number of improvements backstage.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: The small dark dressing rooms we used to have have been converted into two large airy rooms, so they're much more comfortable now.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: And the state-of-the-art electronic sound and lighting systems have been installed.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: Before you hear the rest of the talk, you have some time to look at questions 17 to 20.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: Now listen and answer questions 17 to 20.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: OK, so what's the first play that audiences can see when the theater reopens?
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: We've got a very exciting production of Peter Shaffer's Royal Hunt of the Sun, which is currently touring the country.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: That starts on October the 13th and runs till the 19th.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: We're experimenting a bit with the time the curtain goes up.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: We used to start all our performances at 7:30, but that made it difficult for people to go home by public transport.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: So instead, we're beginning at 7.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: Because at 9:45 when it finishes, there are still buses running.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: Uh huh.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: Tickets are already selling fast, the Friday and Saturday performances sold out almost immediately.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: And in fact now there are only tickets for Monday and Thursday.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: Uh. How much are they?
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: We've introduced a simpler price structure.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: Ticket prices used to range from £6 to £30, but now they're all £18.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: They're available from the box office, in person, by phone, fax or post, or online.
utterances.speaker: LYNNE

utterances.text: OK, Lynne.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Now, if you'd like to give the contact details for the theater, so that our listeners...
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: That is the end of section 2.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

text: What change has been made to each part of the theatre?
textReadable: What change has been made to each part of the theatre?

desc.constraint: NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER

test_text.title: Brian's Inquiries about the Management Course and College Facilities

audioscript.text: Section 3, you will hear a man inquiring about college courses.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 25.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 25.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Hello, can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: I was told to come here because I'd like to talk to someone about taking a management course.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Right, I'm one of the tutors, so I should be able to help you.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Oh, good.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Uh. My name is Brian Ardley.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: I've decided to enroll on a part-time management course, a friend of mine took one last year and recommended it to me.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Right.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Is there anything I should do before the course like reading or anything?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: We prefer to integrate reading with the course, so we don't give out a reading list in advance.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: But we like people to write a case study describing an organization they know.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: I've already done that, as my friends told me you wanted one, but would it be possible to sit in on a teaching session to see what it's like?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Uh. I haven't been a student for quite a while.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Fine, just let me know which date, and I'll arrange it with the tutor.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Now, could I ask you about the college facilities, please?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Anything in particular?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Well, the course is one day a week, all day, isn't it?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: So presumably it's possible to buy food?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Yes, the refectory is open all day.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Does it cater for special diets?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: I have some food allergies.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Provided you warn the refectory in advance, it won't be a problem.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Good.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: W- what about facilities for young children?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: I'd like to bring my daughter here while I'm studying.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: How old is she?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Then she's eligible to join the nursery, which is supervised by a qualified nursery nurse.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: The waiting list for a place is quite long, though, so you ought to apply now.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: OK.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: I don't know if our careers advice service would be of any interest to you.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Yes, it might help me decide how to develop my career after the course.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: The center has a lot of reference materials, and staff qualified to give guidance on a 1 to 1 basis.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: I noticed a fitness center next to the college.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Is that for students?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: It's open to everyone, but students pay an annual fee that's much less than the general public pay.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: And presumably the college library stocks newspapers and journals as well as books.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Yes, and there's also an audio visual room for viewing and listening to videos, cassettes, and so on.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Is there also access to computers?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Yes, your tutor will need to arrange with the technical support team for you to get a password.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: So, ask him or her about it when you start the course.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: OK.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 26 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 26 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: By the way, do you know about our business center?
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: No, what's that?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: It's a training resource, a collection of materials for people to study on their own or use in their own organizations.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Uh huh.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: You mean books and videos?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Yes, and manuals for self study, plus a lot of computer based materials, so people can work through them at their own speed, and repeat anything they aren't sure about.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: And you can hire laptops to use in your own home or workplace, as well as printers that you can take away.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Does it have anything that I could use to improve my study skills?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: I don't have much idea about report writing, and I'm sure I'll need it on the course.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Oh, yes, there's plenty of useful material.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Just ask one of the staff.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Does the center cover all the main areas of business?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Yes, topics like finance and of course marketing, that's a popular one.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Local managers seem to queue up to borrow the videos.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: So, it isn't just for students, then.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: No, it's for members only, but anyone can join.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: How much does it cost?
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: £100 a year for a company, and £50 for an individual with no discount for students, I'm afraid.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Well, that's very helpful.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Well, I think that's all.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: I'd better go home and fill in the enrollment form.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: Thanks for all your help.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: You're welcome.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Goodbye.
audioscript.speaker: TUTOR

audioscript.text: Goodbye.
audioscript.speaker: BRIAN

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

items.title: What is Brian going to do before the course starts?

includes books, journals, equipment room containing audio-visual materials

test_text.title: A Glance at the History and Early 20th Century Life in London's East End

audioscript.text: Section 4, you will hear a talk on a Social History of the East End of London.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: In the last few weeks we've been looking at various aspects of the social history of London, and this morning we are continuing with a look at life in the area called the East End.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: I'll start with a brief history of the district.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And then focus on life in the first half of the 20th century.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Back in the first to the fourth centuries AD, when the Romans controlled England, London grew into a town of 45,000 people, and what's now the East End - the area by the River Thames, and along the road heading northeast from London to the coast - consisted of farmland with crops and livestock, which helped to feed that population.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The Romans left in 410 at the beginning of the fifth century, and from then onwards, the country suffered a series of invasions by tribes from present day Germany and Denmark, the Angles, Saxons and Jutes, many of whom settled in the East End.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The technology they introduced meant that metal and leather goods were produced there for the first time.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And as the East End was by the river, ships could transport goods between there and foreign markets.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: In the eleventh century, in 1066 to be precise, the Normans conquered England.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And during the next few centuries, London became one of the most powerful and prosperous cities in Europe.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The East End benefited from this, and because there were fewer restrictions there than in the city itself, plenty of newcomers settled there from abroad, bringing their skills as workers, merchants or money-lenders during the next few hundred years.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: In the 16th century, the first dock was dug, where ships were constructed, eventually making the East End the focus of massive international trade.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And in the late 16th century,
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: when much of the rest of England was suffering economically, a lot of agricultural workers came to the East End to look for alternative work.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: In the 17th century, the East End was still a series of separate semi-rural settlements.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: There was a shortage of accommodation, so marshland was drained and built on to house the large numbers of people now living there.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: By the 19th century, London was the busiest port in the world.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And this became the main source of employment in the East End.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Those who could afford to live in more pleasant surroundings moved out.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And the area became one where the vast majority of people lived in extreme poverty and suffered from appalling sanitary conditions.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That brief outline takes us to the beginning of the 20th century, and now we'll turn to housing.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: At the beginning of the century, living conditions for the majority of working people in East London were very basic indeed.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Houses were crowded closely together, and usually very badly built, because there was no regulation. But the poor and needy were attracted by the possibility of work, and they had to be housed.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It was the availability rather than the condition of the housing.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That was a major concern for tenants and landlords alike.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Few houses had electricity at this time, so other sources of power were used like coal for the fires which heated perhaps just one room.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Of course, the smoke from these contributed a great deal to the air pollution for which London used to be famous.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: A tiny damp unhealthy house like this might well be occupied by two full families, possibly including several children, grandparents, aunts and uncles.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now, before I go on to health implications of this way of life, I'll say something about food and nutrition.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: Houses were crowded closely together, and usually very badly built, because there was no regulation. But the poor and needy were attracted by the possibility of work, and they had to be housed.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: It was the availability rather than the condition of the housing.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: That was a major concern for tenants and landlords alike.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Few houses had electricity at this time, so other sources of power were used like coal for the fires which heated perhaps just one room.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Of course, the smoke from these contributed a great deal to the air pollution for which London used to be famous.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: A tiny damp unhealthy house like this might well be occupied by two full families, possibly including several children, grandparents, aunts and uncles.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Now, before I go on to health implications of this way of life, I'll say something about food and nutrition.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: That is the end of Section 4.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

body.title: Social history of the East End of London
Which THREE of the following problems are mentioned in connection with 20th century housing in the East End?
